project.name: Example ASC Project
project.site_description: Lot 7, Commerce Park Subdivision Phase I (Davidson Co.)

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.use_when: Default recommendation based on scope and market

lines.desc: Plumbing fixtures, trim, and stops (72 fixtures across 14 types)
Equipment: water heater, TMV, RPZ backflow preventer, expansion tank, hot water recirculation pump, emergency eyewashes, shock arrester
PVC Schedule 40 DWV pipe and fittings, 450 LF below slab
Cast iron no-hub pipe and fittings, 650 LF above slab
Copper Type L pipe and fittings, 900 LF water distribution
Specialty plumbing connections: humidifier, RTU condensate drains, sterilizer drain
Trap primers for floor drains, ASSE 1018 pressure-drop type
Interior storm drainage: roof drains, internal leaders, downspout connections
Plumbing labor: full installation, testing, certification
Domestic water service from city meter to building entry, including RPZ backflow preventer
Sanitary sewer lateral from building to city main (gravity, no lift station)
Natural gas service line from meter to building, including drip leg and shutoff
700 LF black iron Schedule 40 gas piping with supports, penetrations, and pressure tests
Gas pressure regulators on each appliance
Commercial plumbing permit + plan review + inspections; chlorination, flushing, and bacteriological certification
General and administrative overhead and contractor profit

exclusions: Medical gas systems (oxygen, medical air, vacuum, nitrogen, nitrous oxide, WAGD)
HVAC piping and controls (excluding plumbing connections to gas-fired equipment)
Concrete cutting, core drilling, and slab patching beyond allowance
City tap to water main, water meter installation, and meter pit
City tap to sanitary sewer main
Natural gas meter installation and main tap
Outside site storm drainage (civil/site contractor scope)
Trenching and backfill outside building footprint past 200 LF
Electrical power, control wiring, and disconnects to plumbing equipment
Overtime, weekend, and shift premium work
Soil compaction testing and geotechnical investigation
clarifications: Bid based on issued architectural and plumbing drawings plus site-specific overlay, including all addenda received as of bid date
Copper Type L above slab water distribution; cast iron no-hub above slab sanitary; Schedule 40 PVC below slab DWV
Thermostatic mixing valves included at all healthcare patient-area lavatories per applicable guidelines
RPZ backflow preventer at building water service entry included
Initial chlorination, flushing, and bacteriological testing of domestic water included
Bid based on applicable building codes and healthcare facility guidelines (most restrictive adoption)
Base bid held for the validity period from bid date; thereafter subject to material price re-verification

allowances.desc: Owner-furnished equipment receiving, install, and hookup

growth_pitch: Example Health Services has one operating ambulatory surgery center, one under construction, and plans for additional locations across the region. We are positioning to be the preferred plumbing trade partner for the entire build-out program.

differentiators.headline: Why Example Plumbing for This Build
points: Local presence within 15 minutes of the project site
Owner on site daily during plumbing rough-in
Plumbing-only specialist with no divided attention
Full greenfield site scope included in base bid
Transparent risk loading with visible allowances

phone_scripts.opening: Hi, my name is {{bidder.owner}} with {{bidder.name}}. I'm bidding the plumbing on a new ambulatory surgery center at {{project.site_address}}. Project name is {{project.name}}, GC is {{project.gc}}. I have a few quick questions.
questions: For a new commercial healthcare ASC of about {{project.building_size_sf}} square feet, what classification for the sewer connection fee?
For the domestic water service, does the city furnish the tap, meter, and meter box?
Any system development charges or impact fees beyond the tap fee?
phone_scripts.write_down: Sewer fee: $___  Water tap confirmed: $___  City furnishes tap? Y/N  Other fees: ___

phone_scripts.opening: Hi, this is {{bidder.owner}} with {{bidder.name}}. I'm bidding gas piping on a new commercial healthcare building at {{project.site_address}}.
questions: How close is the existing gas main to the property line?
If we need to extend the main, who pays for the extension?
What's the lead time for a new commercial gas service connection?
Is there a meter set fee separate from the tap?
phone_scripts.write_down: Distance to main: ___ ft  Extension needed? Y/N  Who pays: ___  Lead time: ___ weeks  Meter fee: $___

phone_scripts.opening: Hi, this is {{bidder.owner}} with {{bidder.name}}. I'm bidding plumbing on a new healthcare facility at {{project.site_address}}.
questions: For an ASC, do you require an RPZ at the building service entry, or a different spec for healthcare?
What's your initial test fee and the recurring annual test fee?
phone_scripts.write_down: RPZ spec: ___  Initial test: $___  Annual test: $___

phone_scripts.opening: Hi, this is {{bidder.owner}} with {{bidder.name}}, license {{bidder.license}}. I have questions about pulling a commercial plumbing permit at {{project.site_address}}.
questions: Is the property inside city limits or unincorporated county?
Do I need a local plumbing license on file, or does my state license cover it?
phone_scripts.write_down: Jurisdiction: City / County  Local license needed? Y/N  Cost: $___  Permit fee: $___

phone_scripts.opening: Hi, this is {{bidder.owner}} with {{bidder.name}}. I'm bidding underground plumbing on a new commercial site at {{project.site_address}}.
questions: Are there any zoning restrictions or overlay districts that affect underground plumbing routing on this parcel?
phone_scripts.write_down: Restrictions: ___  Setbacks: ___ ft  Permit needed? Y/N

schedule_milestones.target: Per GC schedule, ahead of slab pour

schedule_milestones.milestone: Trim, fixture set, and final connections

schedule_milestones.target: Per GC schedule, before owner walk-through

payment_terms: Monthly progress billing using AIA G702/G703 forms or GC's preferred pay-app form
Net 30 days from receipt of approved pay application
10% retention withheld from each progress payment, released at substantial completion
50% deposit on long-lead equipment at submittal approval, against supplier lock-in pricing
Mechanic's lien rights preserved as provided by law; lien waivers provided concurrent with each pay application
All scope changes require written approval prior to commencement
about: Example Plumbing, LLC is a licensed mechanical contractor based in Nashville, Tennessee. We specialize in commercial new construction and healthcare facility plumbing. John Smith owns and operates the company, holds the state license personally as qualifying agent, and is on every job site during critical path work.
insurance: Certificate of Insurance available on request. General liability + workers' compensation in force. Bondability available upon request.
